retry_reason: The refcount operations themselves never fail; failures (NULL/ERR_PTR) come from filesystem errors or ACL unavailability, which may be transient but are not refcount failures. Retry is left to the caller but is not inherent to the refcount wrapper.
contract_summary: Get-wrapper for posix_acl objects. Returns a pointer to a POSIX ACL with an elevated reference count (refcount). The caller must call posix_acl_release() when done with the ACL. The function handles caching: it first checks cache, then uses the filesystem's get_acl/get_inode_acl method to fetch it if uncached. It ensures race-free caching with sentinel. Returns NULL if ACLs are disabled, or ERR_PTR on filesystem error. The caller must check the return value. The returned ACL is valid until released.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to acquire a reference-counted ACL from cache or filesystem. It returns the ACL with an elevated refcount that the caller must release. Caching and sentinel handling are subordinate to the reference management goal.
judge.retry_reason: The refcount operations themselves never fail; failures (NULL/ERR_PTR) come from filesystem errors or ACL unavailability, which may be transient but are not refcount failures. Retry is left to the caller but is not inherent to the refcount wrapper.

check.reason: Only the return value get operation is valid: the function returns 'acl' after calling posix_acl_dup() (or get_cached_acl()), which increments the refcount on the returned pointer. The other two candidate operations (get on parameter 1, put on parameter 1) are not supported by the code; there is no refcount operation on any parameter of __get_acl.
